content:
# Ruth Chapter 2 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 880-881 | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/RUT_002_page_001.jpg, RUT_002_page_002.jpg

---

**2:1** And-to-Naomi [was] a-kinsman of-her-husband, a-man mighty of-valor, from-[the]-family of-Elimelech, and-his-name [was] Boaz.

**2:2** And-said Ruth the-Moabitess to Naomi, "Let-me-go, please, to-the-field, and-let-me-glean among-the-ears-of-grain after [him] in-whose eyes I-find favor." And-she-said to-her, "Go, my-daughter."

**2:3** And-she-went, and-she-came, and-she-gleaned in-the-field after the-reapers. And-her-chance happened [upon] a-portion of-the-field belonging-to-Boaz, who [was] from-[the]-family of-Elimelech.

**2:4** And-behold, Boaz came from-Beth-lehem, and-he-said to-the-reapers, "YHWH [be] with-you." And-they-said to-him, "May-YHWH-bless-you."

**2:5** And-said Boaz to-his-servant, the-[one]-set over the-reapers, "To-whom [belongs] this young-woman?"

**2:6** And-answered the-servant, the-[one]-set over the-reapers, and-said, "A-young-woman, a-Moabitess she [is], the-[one]-returning with Naomi from-[the]-field of-Moab.

**2:7** And-she-said, 'Let-me-glean, please, and-I-will-gather among-the-sheaves after the-reapers.' And-she-came, and-she-has-stood from-then the-morning and-until now; this [is] her-sitting the-house a-little."

**2:8** And-said Boaz to Ruth, "Have-you-not heard, my-daughter? Do-not-go to-glean in-another field, and-also do-not-pass from-this, and-thus you-shall-cling [tidbaqin] to my-young-women.

**2:9** [Let] your-eyes [be] on the-field which they-reap, and-you-shall-go after-them. Have-I-not commanded the-young-men to-not touch-you? And-when-you-thirst, and-you-shall-go to the-vessels, and-you-shall-drink from-what the-young-men draw."

**2:10** And-she-fell on her-face, and-she-bowed-down to-the-ground, and-she-said to-him, "Why have-I-found favor in-your-eyes, to-take-notice-of-me, and-I [am] a-foreigner?"

**2:11** And-answered Boaz and-said to-her, "It-has-been-told, it-was-told to-me all which you-did with your-mother-in-law after [the]-death of-your-husband, and-you-left your-father and-your-mother and-[the]-land of-your-birth, and-you-went to a-people which you-did-not know yesterday [and the]-third-[day].

**2:12** May-YHWH-repay your-deed, and-may-your-wage be complete from-YHWH, God of-Israel, whom you-have-come to-take-refuge under His-wings."

**2:13** And-she-said, "May-I-find favor in-your-eyes, my-lord, for you-have-comforted-me, and-for you-have-spoken upon [the]-heart of-your-maidservant, and-I -- I-am not as-one of-your-maidservants."

**2:14** And-said to-her Boaz at-time-of the-meal, "Draw-near here, and-you-shall-eat from the-bread, and-dip your-morsel in-the-vinegar." And-she-sat at-[the]-side of-the-reapers, and-he-held-out to-her roasted-grain, and-she-ate, and-she-was-satisfied, and-she-had-left-over.

**2:15** And-she-rose to-glean, and-commanded Boaz his-young-men, saying, "Also between the-sheaves let-her-glean, and-do-not-put-her-to-shame.

**2:16** And-also pulling you-shall-pull-out for-her from the-bundles, and-you-shall-leave, and-she-shall-glean, and-do-not rebuke her."

**2:17** And-she-gleaned in-the-field until the-evening, and-she-beat-out that which she-gleaned, and-it-was about an-ephah of-barley.

**2:18** And-she-carried [it], and-she-came [to] the-city, and-saw her-mother-in-law that which she-gleaned. And-she-brought-out, and-she-gave to-her that which she-had-left-over from-her-being-satisfied.

**2:19** And-said to-her her-mother-in-law, "Where did-you-glean today, and-where did-you-work? May-the-[one]-taking-notice-of-you be blessed." And-she-told to-her-mother-in-law with whom she-worked, and-she-said, "[The]-name of-the-man whom I-worked with-him today [is] Boaz."

**2:20** And-said Naomi to-her-daughter-in-law, "Blessed [is] he to-YHWH, who has-not-abandoned his-lovingkindness [hesed] with the-living and-with the-dead." And-said to-her Naomi, "Near to-us the-man; from-our-kinsman-redeemers [go'alenu] he [is]."

**2:21** And-said Ruth the-Moabitess, "Also indeed he-said to-me, 'With the-young-men who [are] to-me you-shall-cling [tidbaqin], until they-have-finished all the-harvest which [is] to-me.'"

**2:22** And-said Naomi to Ruth her-daughter-in-law, "[It is] good, my-daughter, that you-go-out with his-young-women, and-[that] they-do-not-meet-you in-another field."

**2:23** And-she-clung [vattidbaq] to-[the]-young-women of-Boaz to-glean until [the]-completion of-[the]-harvest of-the-barley and-[the]-harvest of-the-wheat. And-she-dwelt with her-mother-in-law.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **2:1 -- "kinsman"** (מְיֻדָּע): The Leningrad Codex reads מְיֻדָּע (meyudda'), a passive participle meaning "one known [to]" or "acquaintance." This is distinct from the term גֹּאֵל (go'el/kinsman-redeemer) which appears later. The narrator carefully introduces Boaz first as a social connection, not yet as a legal redeemer.

2. **2:1 -- "mighty of valor"** (גִּבּוֹר חַיִל): This phrase characterizes Boaz as a man of substance, wealth, and standing -- not merely physical prowess.

3. **2:7 -- Difficult verse**: The last clause is notoriously difficult. "This [is] her-sitting the-house a-little" (זֶה שִׁבְתָּהּ הַבַּיִת מְעָט) likely means she rested in the shelter only briefly. The Leningrad Codex text is preserved literally here without smoothing.

4. **2:8 -- tidbaqin** (תִּדְבָּקִין): The verb דָּבַק (cling/cleave) recurs from 1:14. Boaz uses the same verb of Ruth clinging to his young women that the narrator used of Ruth clinging to Naomi. The form here has the archaic nun-ending.

5. **2:11 -- Infinitive absolute**: "It-has-been-told, it-was-told" (הֻגֵּד הֻגַּד) -- the infinitive absolute + finite verb construction for emphasis is preserved in the double rendering.

6. **2:12 -- "wings"** (כְּנָפָיו): This term will reappear in 3:9 where Ruth asks Boaz to spread his "wing/skirt" (כְּנָפֶךָ) over her -- creating a deliberate echo linking divine protection to Boaz's role as redeemer.

7. **2:20 -- go'alenu** (גֹּאֲלֵנוּ): "Our kinsman-redeemers." This is the first explicit use of the go'el terminology, the legal institution of clan-based redemption that drives the remainder of the narrative.

8. **2:21 -- "young men"** (הַנְּעָרִים): Ruth says Boaz told her to stay with his "young men" (masculine), but in v. 22 Naomi corrects this to "young women" (נַעֲרוֹתָיו). This subtle detail is preserved in the Leningrad Codex.

-- EMET
